headline: GAME-CHANGER: USA Rare Earth Pays $2.8B for Brazil's Serra Verde — Only Non-Asian Supplier of All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) agreed to acquire Serra Verde Group — owner of Brazil's Pela Ema mine, the only large-scale producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale. The $2.8B deal includes a U.S. government offtake for 100% of neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium, and terbium output, directly challenging China's near-total grip on the rare earth supply chain.

headline: CHINA SILVER BINGE: March Imports Hit Record 836 Tons as Solar Factories and Retail Investors Both Pile In
snippet: China's silver imports hit a record 836 tons in March 2026, driven by solar manufacturers front-loading production ahead of export rebate cuts and retail investors hoarding silver bars as a cheaper alternative to gold above $4,800. The surge is deepening an already tight global market facing its sixth consecutive structural deficit.

headline: GOLD SETTLES 2.25% LOWER at $4,698 as Dollar Firms and Iran Ceasefire Deadline Expires Today
snippet: Gold settled sharply lower at $4,698.40, down 2.25% on the session, as a firming dollar and stalled U.S.-Iran nuclear talks weighed on precious metals. The 14-day ceasefire expires April 22 with no deal in sight — negotiations have collapsed over Iran's refusal to end uranium enrichment or transfer its stockpile of 60%-enriched uranium.

headline: BINARY EVENT: Iran Ceasefire Expires Today — Deal or No Deal Sets the Direction for Gold, Copper, and Oil
snippet: With the U.S.-Iran ceasefire expiring April 22, metals markets face a high-stakes binary outcome: a deal extension would rally copper back toward $6.5/lb and ease oil; a collapse risks resumed Strait of Hormuz closure and a safe-haven spike for gold. Analysts note Iran holds approximately 400kg of 60%-enriched uranium — close to weapons-grade — as the central sticking point in negotiations.

headline: Greg Weldon: STAGFLATION IS HERE NOW — 488,000 Left the Labor Force in March While Inflation Stayed Sticky
snippet: Veteran market analyst Greg Weldon argues stagflation has arrived, not looming: despite a headline March payroll gain, 488,000 people exited the labor force and inflation remains sticky. He sees a weakening dollar, de-dollarization pressure from China, and mounting consumer stress as the macro setup for the next significant leg higher in gold and silver.

headline: COPPER RETREATS From $6/lb Two-Month High as Iran Ceasefire Doubts Return and Hormuz Closes Again
snippet: Copper slipped from a two-month high near $6 per pound after Iran reasserted control of the Strait of Hormuz and ceasefire talks with Washington stalled. The move reverses a 2-week rally driven by peace hopes; analysts note the underlying structural copper supply deficit and smelter margin collapse remain intact regardless of near-term geopolitical noise.

headline: EXPLORATION BOOM: Heraeus Says Record Gold and Silver Prices Are Redirecting $Billions Into Junior Mine Searches
snippet: Precious metals refiner Heraeus reports that sustained record-high prices for gold and silver are now materially accelerating global exploration budgets, with silver projects up 26% in 2025 and economically viable grade projects rising 37%. Half of every exploration dollar globally is now chasing gold — the highest concentration ever recorded.

headline: Gold SLUMP FAILS to Deter Debasement Bets — Institutions Hold Firm in GDX and GDXJ Despite Pullback
snippet: Despite gold's 2%+ pullback from above $4,800, institutional bets on currency debasement via gold mining ETFs are not flinching — GDX is up 108% year-over-year and GDXJ has returned 203% over the same period. Analysts argue that four out of five plausible economic scenarios point to continued de-dollarization, making senior and junior miners the preferred leveraged vehicle.

headline: U.S. WEIGHS $20 BILLION Cash-for-Uranium Deal With Iran as Nuclear Talks Hit Critical Stage
snippet: The U.S. is considering trading $20 billion in frozen Iranian funds for Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium — an offer Tehran has so far rejected. Iran is demanding the right to continue enrichment, while Washington insists on dismantling major enrichment facilities. The uranium sticking point is the central obstacle as the April 22 ceasefire deadline expires today.

headline: Goldman Sachs Resets Gold Price Outlook: PULLBACK FROM $4,800 Creates Entry Point Before Next Leg Higher
snippet: Goldman Sachs delivered an updated gold price message for the remainder of 2026, characterizing the current pullback from $4,800+ as a technical correction within a sustained bull market rather than a trend reversal. The bank continues to cite central bank accumulation and de-dollarization as structural supports for gold through year-end.
